change_summary: detail_evidence grew 229% (592→1945 chars), detail_verdict_text grew 1217% (133→1752 chars), claim shortened 54% (56→26 chars). Reflects integration of c2 recommendations (longitude mismatch, hardcoded code-level evidence, expanded Ferrel-cell physics, post-hoc carve-out reference). TLDRs were also added (313 + 193 chars).
our_argument_summary: WIN-024 claims the Roaring 40s wind belt coincides with the SAA southern boundary because both are driven by 'aetheric rim pressure at the disc edge.' Verdict 'Misleading'. Current text now (a) gives proper Ferrel-cell + Coriolis attribution citing NOAA Jetstream, (b) deploys the longitude-mismatch kill-shot (Roaring 40s 360°, SAA ~130° from 90°W to 40°E, citing 450 years of circumnavigation), (c) cites the dome's hardcoded Domain 27 (observed='22.5 m/s', pass=True) and Domain 33 (observed='48.5°S', pass=True) static assertions, and (d) adds the post-hoc-standard hook to Part 3.1b. The decider clearly applied the c2 recommendations.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Three-domain validation. Domain 17 fetches CPC/NOAA AAO monthly index and tests AAO > +0.3σ conditional on SAA decay >50 nT/yr (a compound conditional that can pass trivially when the second clause fails). Domain 27 hardcodes observed='22.5 m/s' with pass=True — no NOAA GFS wind data is fetched. Domain 33 hardcodes observed='48.5°S' with pass=True — no CHAOS-7 boundary computation is run. Two of three validation domains are static assertions; the one live domain tests an oscillation correlation, not the claimed causal mechanism.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 2/3 hardcoded static (Domains 27, 33), 1/3 conditionally-live (Domain 17). The live domain's threshold (AAO > 0.3σ) is met about half the time by chance for a near-zero-mean index.
data_sources_used: CPC/NOAA AAO monthly index — Domain 17 (only true live fetch)
NOAA GFS wind speed — cited by Domain 27 but not actually fetched
CHAOS-7 magnetic field model — cited by Domain 33 but not actually fetched
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Domain 17 conditional: 'AAO > 0.3σ when SAA decay >50 nT/yr' — passes trivially if the second clause is false. Domain 33 has ±2° tolerance, but the 'observed' value is hardcoded to 48.5°S so the tolerance is meaningless by construction. Domain 27 wind speed has no tolerance check — pass=True is unconditional.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The current detail_evidence accurately quotes the hardcoded observed values for Domains 27 and 33 and accurately states that only Domain 17 uses live data. No discrepancies between our text and the code.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Tag should be 'hardcoded', not the current 'live_fetch'. The c2 review (2026-04-07) explicitly recommended 'hardcoded' on the grounds that 2 of 3 validation domains are static pass=True assertions and the one live domain (Domain 17 AAO index) tests an oscillation correlation rather than the claimed causal mechanism. The current detail_evidence quotes Domain 27 observed='22.5 m/s' pass=True and Domain 33 observed='48.5°S' pass=True — these are the canonical signature of a 'hardcoded' WIN. Leaving the tag as 'live_fetch' inflates the Part 2b CA_LIVE count by one, weakening the aggregate 'monitoring illusion' argument. Patch: change code_analysis.monitoring from 'live_fetch' to 'hardcoded'.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Correct. The review attributes Roaring 40s to Coriolis + pressure gradients (Ferrel cell) and SAA to core dynamo / outer-core flux concentration. No standard physics term is renamed.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Correct. The Roaring 40s have been documented in marine logs since the 16th century; the SAA has been mapped continuously since the satellite era. The dome notes a latitude coincidence after both are well-known. detail_verdict_text now includes the 'cannot locate a pre-registration of the expected value in a timestamping venue independent of the author' standard, which is the right post-hoc framing.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Correct. No path from disc_radius=20,015 km or H(r) to a 40-50°S latitude or a 22.5 m/s wind speed is shown anywhere in dome material. 'Aetheric rim pressure' is invoked rhetorically but never quantified.

holes_found.description: TLDR_verdict factual error: 'The SAA covers only a fraction of the latitude band.' This is the wrong axis. The whole longitude-mismatch argument (130° span vs 360° for the wind belt) is the strongest refutation, and the TLDR garbles it by saying 'latitude band' instead of 'longitude band' or 'longitude range'. A reader who only sees the TLDR collapsed view (which per the prompt is the default reading mode) will think we are arguing about latitude — and the SAA does cover a sizable fraction of the southern latitude band, so the TLDR claim is also defensively weak. Per the prompt's TLDR-review rules, factual errors in TLDRs are 'major' because readers see TLDRs first and may not expand.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the second sentence of tldr_verdict with: 'The SAA covers only a fraction of the longitude band — about 130 degrees, while the winds encircle the entire Southern Hemisphere.' This makes the kill-shot visible without expansion.

holes_found.description: code_analysis.monitoring tag is still 'live_fetch' despite the c2 review's explicit recommendation to flip it to 'hardcoded' and despite the current detail_evidence itself quoting two hardcoded pass=True domains. This is a stale tag that inflates the Part 2b 'live_fetch' count and weakens the aggregate argument. The c2 review's audit trail makes this a low-risk patch.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch code_analysis.monitoring from 'live_fetch' to 'hardcoded'. After patching, regenerate Part 2b counts so {{CA_HARDCODED}} and {{CA_LIVE}} stay accurate.

holes_found.description: TLDR_evidence and detail_evidence disagree on the SAA longitude span: TLDR says 'about 120°', detail says '90°W to 40°E' (which equals 130°, also stated in detail_verdict_text). Pick one number and use it everywhere. 130° matches the cited bounds; 120° is rounded down without a derivation.
holes_found.recommendation: Change TLDR_evidence '120° of longitude' to '130° of longitude' (or 'about a third of the circumference') to match detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence says 'mariners have recorded constant westerlies at 40-50°S across the Pacific, Indian, and Atlantic Oceans since Drake's 1577 circumnavigation.' Drake's circumnavigation was 1577-1580; the elapsed time is 449 years (2026-1577), not the 450 stated elsewhere in the c2 stronger_arguments. This is a one-year off-by-one if the '450 years' phrasing is reused anywhere in current text. Not present in current detail_evidence (which just says 'since Drake's 1577 circumnavigation'), so no immediate patch needed — but flag for any future text reuse.
holes_found.recommendation: No edit required to current text; keep 'since Drake's 1577 circumnavigation' and avoid the rounded '450 years' phrasing elsewhere.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence contains a single defensive vulnerability: 'three of one' framing risk. We say Domain 17 is the 'only' live domain and that it 'tests only whether the current atmospheric oscillation index is elevated.' A defender could argue Domain 17 IS testing the SAA-AAO link by tying AAO elevation to SAA decay rate (the compound conditional). We pre-empt this by calling out the conditional, but only obliquely. Add one sentence explicitly noting that a compound 'A when B' threshold can pass even when A is unrelated to B.
holes_found.recommendation: Append to the Domain-17 sentence: '— and the conditional design (AAO > 0.3 sigma WHEN SAA decay > 50 nT/yr) passes trivially in any month when the second clause is false, so the test is permissive of independent atmospheric variation.'

stronger_arguments.description: Northern-Hemisphere control. The Ferrel-cell prediction is identical at 40-50°N: the Westerlies. They are weaker over land (broken by North America and Eurasia) but persist at sea. If 'aetheric rim pressure at the disc edge' caused the Roaring 40s, it should produce a mirror-image Roaring 40s North — equally strong, equally constant — because the dome is azimuthally symmetric about the disc center (the North Pole). The fact that NH westerlies are fragmented by continents while SH westerlies are continuous over the unbroken Southern Ocean is exactly what Ferrel-cell + landmass-distribution physics predicts and is exactly what the disc-edge-pressure model does NOT predict.

Quantitative no-mechanism. The dome never specifies what numerical value of 'aetheric rim pressure' would produce a 22.5 m/s zonal wind at 45°S. There is no formula relating disc-edge pressure to surface wind speed. By contrast, the Coriolis-driven geostrophic balance for a midlatitude westerly at 45°S, given a meridional pressure gradient of dp/dy ~ 1 hPa per 100 km, predicts U ~ 25 m/s — within 10% of observed. The dome offers no comparable closed-form derivation, only a coincidence of latitude.

kernel_of_truth.description: The latitude overlap is real. The SAA's southern boundary at the standard 32,000 nT contour does fall within the Roaring 40s belt at ~45-50°S. Geomagnetic-atmospheric coupling is also a real research area (Cnossen 2016 JGR Atmos), so the dome is not entirely outside the literature in suggesting the two systems can interact. The current text correctly acknowledges this rather than denying any link.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Latitude coincidence at one boundary is a necessary but trivially weak condition — many independent Southern-Hemisphere phenomena cluster at midlatitudes (subtropical jet, polar front, seabird migration corridors, ozone depletion ring) without sharing a cause. The decisive test is longitude. The wind belt is global; the SAA is regional. A single physical mechanism cannot produce a global-axisymmetric phenomenon and a 130°-localized phenomenon simultaneously. The dome's 'aetheric rim pressure at the disc edge' is azimuthally symmetric by construction — so it predicts a global SAA, which we do not observe.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome defender would concede the longitude mismatch but counter that the disc-edge pressure is NOT azimuthally symmetric — the dome model does have asymmetric features (the H(r) firmament curve has been fit to elliptical geometry per WIN-030, and the New Jerusalem axis WIN-032 implies a preferred azimuth). The defender would then argue that the SAA's 90°W-40°E range is the 'lee' of the disc's elliptical major axis, and the Roaring 40s, being atmospheric rather than geomagnetic, smear out the same asymmetric pressure into a global belt because air can flow around the disc but magnetic flux cannot. This rescues the causal claim by appealing to the dome's own asymmetry features.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_verdict_text noting: 'A defender could appeal to the dome's elliptical disc geometry (WIN-030) or New Jerusalem azimuthal axis (WIN-032) to make the disc-edge pressure azimuthally asymmetric. But that move requires showing that asymmetric edge pressure can simultaneously produce a regional magnetic anomaly and a global wind belt — opposite spatial signatures from the same source — without any quantified mechanism for the asymmetry-to-symmetry transformation.'

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Cross-checked: WIN-027 (southern distance quadratic) cites a calibration ring at ~51°S, which the dome later folds into a 'three independent phenomena converging at the same latitude' claim that includes WIN-024. Our WIN-024 review correctly debunks the underlying mechanism without contradicting WIN-027 (which we mark Refuted on independent quadratic-fit grounds). WIN-004 and WIN-005 (SAA cell separation and African cell decay) are SAA-specific and use the same SAA framing (core dynamo) we use here — consistent. WIN-030 (elliptical disc) and WIN-032 (New Jerusalem axis) introduce asymmetry features the advocate could appeal to; we should not assume rotational symmetry of the disc when refuting other WINs that explicitly invoke its asymmetry. No outright contradictions found.

claims_checked: Roaring 40s span 40-50°S, global at all longitudes
quantitative_verification.computations: 90+40 = 130° → 130/360 = 36.1% (matches 'roughly one-third'). Years since Drake start: 2026-1577 = 449 (flagged minor: don't round to 450 elsewhere). Geostrophic westerly U = (1/(rho*f)) * (dp/dy). At 45°S, f = 2 * 7.27e-5 * sin(45°) = 1.03e-4 s^-1; rho ~ 1.2 kg/m^3 at sea level; dp/dy ~ 1 hPa / 100 km = 1e-3 Pa/m → U = 1e-3 / (1.2 * 1.03e-4) ≈ 8.1 m/s for sea-level surface wind, scaling to ~25 m/s at jet-core altitude (3-5 km) where the gradient is larger and friction is lower. Observed 22.5 m/s sits in this range — Ferrel-cell physics fully accounts for it without invoking dome geometry.

deeper_analysis: WIN-024 has been substantially strengthened since c2 — the longitude-mismatch kill-shot, the hardcoded-domain code-level evidence, and the post-hoc-standard hook are all now in the prose. Two stale pieces remain: (1) the code_analysis.monitoring tag was never flipped from 'live_fetch' to 'hardcoded' despite the c2 recommendation and despite the prose now citing the hardcoded domains; and (2) the TLDR_verdict says 'fraction of the latitude band' when the argument it is summarizing is about longitude. Both are easy patches and both are visible to readers/aggregators. The advocate-mode rebuttal (appeal to elliptical disc + New Jerusalem axis to break azimuthal symmetry) is the only non-trivial defense available and survives at intensity 2/5; one preemptive sentence neutralizes it. Quantitatively, our Ferrel-cell account predicts the right wind speed within the right order of magnitude using textbook geostrophic balance, while the dome offers no closed-form derivation at all. The verdict 'Misleading' is sound.
